STA 363 Fall 2025

Take-Home Exam 1 Instructions

Due: Friday, October 31st, at 3:00 pm

Rules

  1. Your solutions must be written up in a Quarto (qmd) file and then rendered into an HTML file called exam-01.html. This file must include your code, output, and write-up for each question. Please use the head function when printing any result with over 20 rows.

  2. This exam is open-book, open-internet, and closed to other people. You may use any online or book-based resource you would like, but you must include citations for any code you use (directly or indirectly). You may not consult anyone about this exam other than the Professor. You cannot ask direct questions online, or consult other students, even for a hypothetical question.

  3. You will be required to upload the HTML file from your output. Technical difficulties are not an excuse for late work - do not wait until the last minute. Verify your HTML file includes all graphs and tables before uploading to Moodle. Use the embedded resources option in the YAML. If I have you re-render, and resubmit, you will lose 5 points on the exam. Use the following YAML in your document so images are embedded:

  1. Your analysis, outputs, and narratives, not your code, should answer the questions. I have enabled code folding, so your code will stay collapsed in your rendered documents.

  2. The in-class exam is worth 125 points. The take-home is worth 75 points.

Data

Count data for three species of pygoscelid penguins at three sites near Anvers Island, Antarctica. The three sites are Neko Harbor, Petermann Island, and the Orne Islands, and the data included spans the period between 1984 and 2019. Data was accessed using the online Mapping Application for Penguin Populations and Projected Dynamics. Citation for MAPPPD:

Humphries G.R.W., Che-Castaldo C., Naveen R., Schwaller M., McDowall P., Schrimpf M., and Lynch H.J. 2017. Mapping Application for Penguin Populations and Projected Dynamics (MAPPPD): Data and tools for dynamic management and decision support. Polar Records

Data article.

Data (Penguin_Count_data.xlsx) and a corresponding metadata file (penguin_count_data.meta.txt) can be found in the shared class folder on the RStudio server. You will need to move the data into your own exam project for the above code to run.

Questions

  1. Complete an exploratory data analysis. The code provided should be helpful, but it is not exhaustive (more may be needed). You do not need to replicate any of the included EDA in your script. Please reference the included plots using the figure numbers.

  2. Fit appropriate regression models to predict the penguin count. Explain your process and the decisions you make, such as when you create a new variable or drop a variable.

  3. Compare the models and choose the best model. Use statistical tests and metrics where possible and as appropriate. Model conditions may influence your decision.

  4. Assess your final chosen model. Check model condition for your final model and discuss any use limitations.

  5. Interpret coefficients from your chosen model. Interpret all of the coefficients in your final model.

Submission

When you are finished with your exam, render the final document. Once rendered, you can download your file by:

  • Finding the .html file in your File pane (on the bottom right of the screen)
  • Click the check box next to the file
  • Click the blue gear above and then click “Export” to download
  • Submit your final html document to the exam spot on Moodle
